title: WHO guideline for non-surgical management of chronic primary low back pain in adults in primary and community care settings
notes: Primary global guideline; includes recommended and not recommended intervention classes and emphasizes a person-centred, biopsychosocial approach.

title: Summary brochure: WHO guideline for chronic primary low back pain
notes: Two-page quick reference listing intervention classes; used for in-app education cards.

title: Low back pain and sciatica in over 16s: assessment and management (NG59)
authors: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE)
journal: NICE Guideline (updated 2020; PDF accessible via NICE)
notes: Key points used: self-management advice, encourage normal activity, exercise programs, cautious imaging approach.

title: Noninvasive Treatments for Acute, Subacute, and Chronic Low Back Pain: A Clinical Practice Guideline from the American College of Physicians
authors: Qaseem A, Wilt TJ, McLean RM, Forciea MA; Clinical Guidelines Committee of the American College of Physicians
journal: Annals of Internal Medicine (ACP guideline PDF)
notes: Key points: prioritize non-drug therapies; exercise, mind-body approaches for chronic LBP; superficial heat for acute/subacute; avoid unnecessary interventions.

title: Interventions for the Management of Acute and Chronic Low Back Pain: Revision 2021
authors: George SZ, Fritz JM, Silfies SP, Schneider MJ, Beneciuk JM, Lentz TA, Gilliam JR, Hendren S, Norman KS
journal: Journal of Orthopaedic & Sports Physical Therapy (Clinical Practice Guideline)
notes: PT-focused CPG; supports education and exercise categories (motor control / trunk coordination / general exercise), with decision-tree guidance.

title: Low Back Pain Decision Tree (CPG Revision 2021)
notes: Algorithmic summary of the 2021 CPG; used to inspire app’s screening and pathway logic (not diagnostic).

title: Rehabilitation Protocol for Conservative Management of Low Back Pain With Mobility Deficits
notes: Pragmatic phased rehab protocol; informs the app’s phase structure and common exercise choices.

title: Exercise for treatment of chronic low back pain (Cochrane evidence)
notes: Evidence summary that exercise can reduce pain and improve function in chronic LBP compared to minimal care; certainty varies.

title: Cochrane for Clinicians: Is exercise effective for adults with chronic low back pain?
notes: Clinician-friendly summary: exercise reduces pain and improves function vs usual care, especially short-term.

title: The effect and mechanism of motor control exercise on low back pain: a narrative review
authors: Xu H-R, Zhang Y-H, Zheng Y-L
notes: Open-access review discussing motor control/stabilisation exercise mechanisms and evidence; used for motor-control education cards.

title: Yoga compared to non-exercise or physical therapy exercise on pain and functional disability in chronic low back pain: A meta-analysis
notes: Open-access meta-analysis; yoga may reduce pain short-to-intermediate term and improve disability vs non-exercise; certainty varies.

title: Effectiveness and cost-effectiveness of an individualised, progressive walking and education intervention for the prevention of low back pain recurrence in Australia (WalkBack): a randomised controlled trial
notes: Large RCT: walking + education reduced recurrence risk; informs the app’s walking progression module.

title: Back pain: When to see a doctor
notes: Used to define safety red flags: bowel/bladder issues, fever, severe night pain, weakness/numbness, etc.

title: Cauda Equina Syndrome – Important Warning Signs (patient leaflet)
notes: Used for the app’s cauda equina red-flag education and safety gate.
